book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
On the Clause, And Shall Come in Glory to Judge the Quick and the Dead; Of Whose Kingdom There Shall Be No End.

ccel.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

title: On the Clause, And Shall Come in Glory to Judge the Quick and the Dead; Of Whose Kingdom There Shall Be No End.

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I beheld till thrones were placed, and one that was ancient of days did sit, and then, I saw in a vision of the night, and behold one like unto the Son of Man came with the clouds of heaven, &c.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We preach not one advent only of Christ, but a second also, far more glorious than the former.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the former gave a view of His patience; but the latter brings with it the crown of a divine kingdom.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For all things, for the most part, are twofold in our Lord Jesus Christ: a twofold generation; one, of God, before the ages; and one, of a Virgin, at the close of the ages: His descents twofold; one, the unobserved, like rain on a fleece; and a second His open coming, which is to be.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In His former advent, He was wrapped in swaddling clothes in the manger; in His second, He covereth Himself with light as with a garment.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In His first coming, He endured the Cross, despising shame; in His second, He comes attended by a host of Angels, receiving glory.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We rest not then upon His first advent only, but look also for His second.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And as at His first coming we said, Blessed is He that cometh in the Name of the Lord, so will we repeat the same at His second coming; that when with Angels we meet our Master, we may worship Him and say, Blessed is He that cometh in the Name of the Lord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Saviour comes, not to be judged again, but to judge them who judged Him; He who before held His peace when judged, shall remind the transgressors who did those daring deeds at the Cross, and shall say, These things hast thou done, and I kept silence.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then, He came because of a divine dispensation, teaching men with persuasion; but this time they will of necessity have Him for their King, even though they wish it not.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And concerning these two comings, Malachi the Prophet says, And the Lord whom ye seek shall suddenly come to His temple; behold one coming.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again of the second coming he says, And the Messenger of the covenant whom ye delight in.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Behold, He cometh, saith the Lord Almighty.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But who shall abide the day of His coming? or who shall stand when He appeareth?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Because He cometh in like a refiner’s fire, and like fullers’ herb; and He shall sit as a refiner and purifier.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And immediately after the Saviour Himself says, And I will draw near to you in judgment; and I will be a swift witness against the sorcerers, and against the adulteresses, and against those who swear falsely in My Name, and the rest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For this cause Paul warning us beforehand says, If any man buildeth on the foundation gold, and silver, and precious stones, wood, hay, stubble; every man’s work shall be made manifest; for the day shall declare it, because it shall be revealed in fire.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Paul also knew these two comings, when writing to Titus and saying, The grace of God hath appeared which bringeth salvation unto all men, instructing us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, and godly, and righteously in this present world; looking for the blessed hope, and appearing of the glory of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest how he spoke of a first, for which he gives thanks; and of a second, to which we look forward.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Therefore the words also of the Faith which we are announcing were just now delivered thus; that we believe in Him, who also ascended into the heavens, and sat down on the right hand of the Father, and shall come in glory to judge quick and dead; whose kingdom shall have no end.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Our Lord Jesus Christ, then, comes from heaven; and He comes with glory at the end of this world, in the last day.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For of this world there is to be an end, and this created world is to be re-made anew.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For since corruption, and theft, and adultery, and every sort of sins have been poured forth over the earth, and blood has been mingled with blood in the world, therefore, that this wondrous dwelling-place may not remain filled with iniquity, this world passeth away, that the fairer world may be made manifest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And wouldest thou receive the proof of this out of the words of Scripture?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Listen to Esaias, saying, And the heaven shall be rolled together as a scroll; and all the stars shall fall, as leaves from a vine, and as leaves fall from a fig-tree.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Gospel also says, The sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the stars shall fall from heaven.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us not sorrow, as if we alone died; the stars also shall die; but perhaps rise again.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the Lord rolleth up the heavens, not that He may destroy them, but that He may raise them up again more beautiful.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hear David the Prophet saying, Thou, Lord, in the beginning didst lay the foundations of the earth, and the heavens are the work of Thy hands; they shall perish, but Thou remainest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But some one will say, Behold, he says plainly that they shall perish.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hear in what sense he says, they shall perish; it is plain from what follows; And they all shall wax old as doth a garment; and as a vesture shalt Thou fold them up, and they shall be changed.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For as a man is said to “perish,” according to that which is written, Behold, how the righteous perisheth, and no man layeth it to heart, and this, though the resurrection is looked for; so we look for a resurrection, as it were, of the heavens also.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The sun shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Here let converts from the Manichees gain instruction, and no longer make those lights their gods; nor impiously think, that this sun which shall be darkened is Christ.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again hear the Lord saying, Heaven and earth shall pass away, but My words shall not pass away; for the creatures are not as precious as the Master’s words.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The things then which are seen shall pass away, and there shall come the things which are looked for, things fairer than the present; but as to the time let no one be curious.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For it is not for you, He says, to know times or seasons, which the Father hath put in His own power.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And venture not thou to declare when these things shall be, nor on the other hand supinely slumber.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For he saith, Watch, for in such an hour as ye expect not the Son of Man cometh.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But since it was needful for us to know the signs of the end, and since we are looking for Christ, therefore, that we may not die deceived and be led astray by that false Antichrist, the Apostles, moved by the divine will, address themselves by a providential arrangement to the True Teacher, and say, Tell us, when shall these things be, and what shall be the sign of Thy coming, and of the end of the world?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We look for Thee to come again, but Satan transforms himself into an Angel of light; put us therefore on our guard, that we may not worship another instead of Thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And He, opening His divine and blessed mouth, says, Take heed that no man mislead you.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Do you also, my hearers, as seeing Him now with the eyes of your mind, hear Him saying the same things to you; Take heed that no man mislead you.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And this word exhorts you all to give heed to what is spoken; for it is not a history of things gone by, but a prophecy of things future, and which will surely come.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Not that we prophesy, for we are unworthy; but that the things which are written will be set before you, and the signs declared.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Observe thou, which of them have already come to pass, and which yet remain; and make thyself safe.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Take heed that no man mislead you: for many shall come in My name, saying, I am Christ, and shall mislead many.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This has happened in part: for already Simon Magus has said this, and Menander, and some others of the godless leaders of heresy; and others will say it in our days, or after us.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A second sign.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is there then at this time war between Persians and Romans for Mesopotamia, or no?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Does nation rise up against nation and kingdom against kingdom, or no?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And there shall be famines and pestilences and earthquakes in divers places.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: These things have already come to pass; and again, And fearful sights from heaven, and mighty storms.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Watch therefore, He says; for ye know not at what hour your Lord doth come.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But we seek our own sign of His coming; we Churchmen seek a sign proper to the Church.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the Saviour says, And then shall many be offended, and shall betray one another, and shall hate one another.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If thou hear that bishops advance against bishops, and clergy against clergy, and laity against laity even unto blood, be not troubled; for it has been written before.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Heed not the things now happening, but the things which are written; and even though I who teach thee perish, thou shalt not also perish with me; nay, even a hearer may become better than his teacher, and he who came last may be first, since even those about the eleventh hour the Master receives.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If among Apostles there was found treason, dost thou wonder that hatred of brethren is found among bishops?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the sign concerns not only rulers, but the people also; for He says, And because iniquity shall abound, the love of the many shall wax cold.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Will any then among those present boast that he entertains friendship unfeigned towards his neighbour?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Do not the lips often kiss, and the countenance smile, and the eyes brighten forsooth, while the heart is planning guile, and the man is plotting mischief with words of peace?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast this sign also: And this Gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world for a witness unto all nations, and then shall the end come.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And as we see, nearly the whole world is now filled with the doctrine of Christ.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And what comes to pass after this?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He says next, When therefore ye see the abomination of desolation, which was spoken of by Daniel the Prophet, standing in the Holy Place, let him that readeth understand.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again, Then if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is the Christ, or, Lo, there; believe it not.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hatred of the brethren makes room next for Antichrist; for the devil prepares beforehand the divisions among the people, that he who is to come may be acceptable to them.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But God forbid that any of Christ’s servants here, or elsewhere, should run over to the enemy!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Writing concerning this matter, the Apostle Paul gave a manifest sign, saying, For that day shall not come, except there came first the falling away, and the man of sin be revealed, the son of perdition, who opposeth and exalteth himself against all that is called God, or that is worshipped; so that he sitteth in the temple of God, shewing himself that he is God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Remember ye not that when I was yet with you, I told you these things?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And now ye know that which restraineth, to the end that he may be revealed in his own season.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the mystery of iniquity doth already work, only there is one that restraineth now, until he be taken out of the way.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And then shall the lawless one be revealed, whom the Lord Jesus shall slay with the breath of His mouth, and shall destroy with the brightness of His coming.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Even him, whose coming is after the working of Satan, with all power and signs and lying wonders, and with all deceit of unrighteousness for them that are perishing.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thus wrote Paul, and now is the falling away.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For men have fallen away from the right faith; and some preach the identity of the Son with the Father, and others dare to say that Christ was brought into being out of nothing.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And formerly the heretics were manifest; but now the Church is filled with heretics in disguise.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For men have fallen away from the truth, and have itching ears.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is it a plausible discourse? all listen to it gladly.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is it a word of correction? all turn away from it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Most have departed from right words, and rather choose the evil, than desire the good.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This therefore is the falling away, and the enemy is soon to be looked for: and meanwhile he has in part begun to send forth his own forerunners, that he may then come prepared upon the prey.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Look therefore to thyself, O man, and make safe thy soul.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Church now charges thee before the Living God; she declares to thee the things concerning Antichrist before they arrive.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Whether they will happen in thy time we know not, or whether they will happen after thee we know not; but it is well that, knowing these things, thou shouldest make thyself secure beforehand.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The true Christ, the Only-begotten Son of God, comes no more from the earth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If any come making false shows in the wilderness, go not forth; if they say, Lo, here is the Christ, Lo, there, believe it not.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Look no longer downwards and to the earth; for the Lord descends from heaven; not alone as before, but with many, escorted by tens of thousands of Angels; nor secretly as the dew on the fleece; but shining forth openly as the lightning.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He hath said Himself, As the lightning cometh out of the east, and shineth even unto the west, so shall also the coming of the Son of Man be; and again, And they shall see the Son of Man coming upon the clouds with power and great glory, and He shall send forth His Angels with a great trumpet; and the rest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But as, when formerly He was to take man’s nature, and God was expected to be born of a Virgin, the devil created prejudice against this, by craftily preparing among idol-worshippers fables of false gods, begetting and begotten of women, that, the falsehood having come first, the truth, as he supposed, might be disbelieved; so now, since the true Christ is to come a second time, the adversary, taking occasion by the expectation of the simple, and especially of them of the circumcision, brings in a certain man who is a magician, and most expert in sorceries and enchantments of beguiling craftiness; who shall seize for himself the power of the Roman empire, and shall falsely style himself Christ; by this name of Christ deceiving the Jews, who are looking for the Anointed, and seducing those of the Gentiles by his magical illusions.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But this aforesaid Antichrist is to come when the times of the Roman empire shall have been fulfilled, and the end of the world is now drawing near.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: There shall rise up together ten kings of the Romans, reigning in different parts perhaps, but all about the same time; and after these an eleventh, the Antichrist, who by his magical craft shall seize upon the Roman power; and of the kings who reigned before him, three he shall humble, and the remaining seven he shall keep in subjection to himself.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: At first indeed he will put on a show of mildness (as though he were a learned and discreet person), and of soberness and benevolence: and by the lying signs and wonders of his magical deceit having beguiled the Jews, as though he were the expected Christ, he shall afterwards be characterized by all kinds of crimes of inhumanity and lawlessness, so as to outdo all unrighteous and ungodly men who have gone before him; displaying against all men, but especially against us Christians, a spirit murderous and most cruel, merciless and crafty.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And after perpetrating such things for three years and six months only, he shall be destroyed by the glorious second advent from heaven of the only-begotten Son of God, our Lord and Saviour Jesus, the true Christ, who shall slay Antichrist with the breath of His mouth, and shall deliver him over to the fire of hell.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now these things we teach, not of our own invention, but having learned them out of the divine Scriptures used in the Church, and chiefly from the prophecy of Daniel just now read; as Gabriel also the Archangel interpreted it, speaking thus: The fourth beast shall be a fourth kingdom upon earth, which shall surpass all kingdoms.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And that this kingdom is that of the Romans, has been the tradition of the Church’s interpreters.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For as the first kingdom which became renowned was that of the Assyrians, and the second, that of the Medes and Persians together, and after these, that of the Macedonians was the third, so the fourth kingdom now is that of the Romans.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then Gabriel goes on to interpret, saying, His ten horns are ten kings that shall arise; and another king shall rise up after them, who shall surpass in wickedness all who were before him; (he says, not only the ten, but also all who have been before him;) and he shall subdue three kings; manifestly out of the ten former kings: but it is plain that by subduing three of these ten, he will become the eighth king; and he shall speak words against the Most High.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A blasphemer the man is and lawless, not having received the kingdom from his fathers, but having usurped the power by means of sorcery.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And who is this, and from what sort of working?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Interpret to us, O Paul.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Whose coming, he says, is after the working of Satan, with all power and signs and lying wonders; implying, that Satan has used him as an instrument, working in his own person through him; for knowing that his judgment shall now no longer have respite, he wages war no more by his ministers, as is his wont, but henceforth by himself more openly.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And with all signs and lying wonders; for the father of falsehood will make a show of the works of falsehood, that the multitudes may think that they see a dead man raised, who is not raised, and lame men walking, and blind men seeing, when the cure has not been wrought.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again he says, Who opposeth and exalteth himself against all that is called God, or that is worshipped; (against every God; Antichrist forsooth will abhor the idols,) so that he seateth himself in the temple of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What temple then?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He means, the Temple of the Jews which has been destroyed.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For God forbid that it should be the one in which we are!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Why say we this?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: That we may not be supposed to favour ourselves.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if he comes to the Jews as Christ, and desires to be worshipped by the Jews, he will make great account of the Temple, that he may more completely beguile them; making it supposed that he is the man of the race of David, who shall build up the Temple which was erected by Solomon.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And Antichrist will come at the time when there shall not be left one stone upon another in the Temple of the Jews, according to the doom pronounced by our Saviour; for when, either decay of time, or demolition ensuing on pretence of new buildings, or from any other causes, shall have overthrown all the stones, I mean not merely of the outer circuit, but of the inner shrine also, where the Cherubim were, then shall he come with all signs and lying wonders, exalting himself against all idols; at first indeed making a pretence of benevolence, but afterwards displaying his relentless temper, and that chiefly against the Saints of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For he says, I beheld, and the same horn made war with the saints; and again elsewhere, there shall be a time of trouble, such as never was since there was a nation upon earth, even to that same time.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Dreadful is that beast, a mighty dragon, unconquerable by man, ready to devour; concerning whom though we have more things to speak out of the divine Scriptures, yet we will content ourselves at present with thus much, in order to keep within compass.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For this cause the Lord knowing the greatness of the adversary grants indulgence to the godly, saying, Then let them which be in Judæa flee to the mountains.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But if any man is conscious that he is very stout-hearted, to encounter Satan, let him stand (for I do not despair of the Church’s nerves), and let him say, Who shall separate us from the love of Christ and the rest?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But, let those of us who are fearful provide for our own safety; and those who are of a good courage, stand fast: for then shall be great tribulation, such as hath not been from the beginning of the world until now, no, nor ever shall be.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But thanks be to God who hath confined the greatness of that tribulation to a few days; for He says, But for the elect’s sake those days shall be shortened; and Antichrist shall reign for three years and a half only.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We speak not from apocryphal books, but from Daniel; for he says, And they shall be given into his hand until a time and times and half a time.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A time is the one year in which his coming shall for a while have increase; and the times are the remaining two years of iniquity, making up the sum of the three years; and the half a time is the six months.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again in another place Daniel says the same thing, And he sware by Him that liveth for ever that it shall be for a time, and times, and half a time.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And some peradventure have referred what follows also to this; namely, a thousand two hundred and ninety days; and this, Blessed is he that endureth and cometh to the thousand three hundred and five and thirty days.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For this cause we must hide ourselves and flee; for perhaps we shall not have gone over the cities of Israel, till the Son of Man be come.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Who then is the blessed man, that shall at that time devoutly witness for Christ?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For I say that the Martyrs of that time excel all martyrs.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the Martyrs hitherto have wrestled with men only; but in the time of Antichrist they shall do battle with Satan in his own person.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And former persecuting kings only put to death; they did not pretend to raise the dead, nor did they make false shows of signs and wonders.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But in his time there shall be the evil inducement both of fear and of deceit, so that if it be possible the very elect shall be deceived.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let it never enter into the heart of any then alive to ask, “What did Christ more?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For by what power does this man work these things?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Were it not God’s will, He would not have allowed them.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Apostle warns thee, and says beforehand, And for this cause God shall send them a working of error; (send, that is, shall allow to happen;) not that they might make excuse, but that they might be condemned.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They, he says, who believed not the truth, that is, the true Christ, but had pleasure in unrighteousness, that is, in Antichrist.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But as in the persecutions which happen from time to time, so also then God will permit these things, not because He wants power to hinder them, but because according to His wont He will through patience crown His own champions like as He did His Prophets and Apostles; to the end that having toiled for a little while they may inherit the eternal kingdom of heaven, according to that which Daniel says, And at that time thy people shall be delivered, every one that shall be found written in the book (manifestly, the book of life); and many of them that sleep in the dust of the earth shall awake, some to everlasting life, and same to shame and everlasting contempt; and they that be wise shall shine as the brightness of the firmament; and of the many righteous, as the stars for ever and ever.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Guard thyself then, O man; thou hast the signs of Antichrist; and remember them not only thyself, but impart them also freely to all.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If thou hast a child according to the flesh, admonish him of this now; if thou hast begotten one through catechizing, put him also on his guard, lest he receive the false one as the True.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the mystery of iniquity doth already work.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I fear these wars of the nations; I fear the schisms of the Churches; I fear the mutual hatred of the brethren.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But enough on this subject; only God forbid that it should be fulfilled in our days; nevertheless, let us be on our guard.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And thus much concerning Antichrist.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But let us wait and look for the Lord’s coming upon the clouds from heaven.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then shall Angelic trumpets sound; the dead in Christ shall rise first,—the godly persons who are alive shall be caught up in the clouds, receiving as the reward of their labours more than human honour, inasmuch as theirs was a more than human strife; according as the Apostle Paul writes, saying, For the Lord Himself shall descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the Archangel, and with the trump of God: and the dead in Christ shall rise first.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then we which are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air; and so shall we ever be with the Lord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This coming of the Lord, and the end of the world, were known to the Preacher; who says, Rejoice, O young man, in thy youth, and the rest; Therefore remove anger from thy heart, and put away evil from thy flesh;…and remember thy Creator…or ever the evil days come,.…or ever the sun, and the light, and the moon, and the stars be darkened,.…and they that look out of the windows be darkened; (signifying the faculty of sight;) or ever the silver cord be loosed; (meaning the assemblage of the stars, for their appearance is like silver;) and the flower of gold be broken; (thus veiling the mention of the golden sun; for the camomile is a well-known plant, having many ray-like leaves shooting out round it;) and they shall rise up at the voice of the sparrow, yea, they shall look away from the height, and terrors shall be in the way.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What shall they see?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then shall they see the Son of man coming on the clouds of heaven; and they shall mourn tribe by tribe.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And what shall come to pass when the Lord is come?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The almond tree shall blossom, and the grasshopper shall grow heavy, and the caper-berry shall be scattered abroad.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And as the interpreters say, the blossoming almond signifies the departure of winter; and our bodies shall then after the winter blossom with a heavenly flower.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the grasshopper shall grow in substance (that means the winged soul clothing itself with the body,) and the caper-berry shall be scattered abroad (that is, the transgressors who are like thorns shall be scattered).

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest how they all foretell the coming of the Lord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest how they know the voice of the sparrow.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us know what sort of voice this is.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the Lord Himself shall descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the Archangel, and with the trump of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Archangel shall make proclamation and say to all, Arise to meet the Lord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And fearful will be that descent of our Master.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: David says, God shall manifestly come, even our God, and shall not keep silence; a fire shall burn before Him, and a fierce tempest round about Him, and the rest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Son of Man shall come to the Father, according to the Scripture which was just now read, on the clouds of heaven, drawn by a stream of fire, which is to make trial of men.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then if any man’s works are of gold, he shall be made brighter; if any man’s course of life be like stubble, and unsubstantial, it shall be burnt up by the fire.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the Father shall sit, having His garment white as snow, and the hair of His head like pure wool.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But this is spoken after the manner of men; wherefore?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Because He is the King of those who have not been defiled with sins; for, He says, I will make your sins white as snow, and as wool, which is an emblem of forgiveness of sins, or of sinlessness itself.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the Lord who shall come from heaven on the clouds, is He who ascended on the clouds; for He Himself hath said, And they shall see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven, with power and great glory.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But what is the sign of His coming? lest a hostile power dare to counterfeit it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And then shall appear, He says, the sign of the Son of Man in heaven.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now Christ’s own true sign is the Cross; a sign of a luminous Cross shall go before the King, plainly declaring Him who was formerly crucified: that the Jews who before pierced Him and plotted against Him, when they see it, may mourn tribe by tribe, saying, “This is He who was buffeted, this is He whose face they spat on, this is He whom they bound with chains, this is He whom of old they crucified, and set at nought.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Whither, they will say, shall we flee from the face of Thy wrath?”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the Angel hosts shall encompass them, so that they shall not be able to flee anywhere.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The sign of the Cross shall be a terror to His foes; but joy to His friends who have believed in Him, or preached Him, or suffered for His sake.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Who then is the happy man, who shall then be found a friend of Christ?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: That King, so great and glorious, attended by the Angel-guards, the partner of the Father’s throne, will not despise His own servants.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For that His Elect may not be confused with His foes, He shall send forth His Angels with a great trumpet, and they shall gather together His elect from the four winds.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He despised not Lot, who was but one; how then shall He despise many righteous?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Come, ye blessed of My Father, will He say to them who shall then ride on chariots of clouds, and be assembled by Angels.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But some one present will say, “I am a poor man,” or again, “I shall perhaps be found at that time sick in bed;” or, “I am but a woman, and I shall be taken at the mill: shall we then be despised?”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Be of good courage, O man; the Judge is no respecter of persons; He will not judge according to a man’s appearance, nor reprove according to his speech.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He honours not the learned before the simple, nor the rich before the needy.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Though thou be in the field, the Angels shall take thee; think not that He will take the landowners, and leave thee the husbandman.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Though thou be a slave, though thou be poor, be not any whit distressed; He who took the form of a servant despises not servants.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Though thou be lying sick in bed, yet it is written, Then shall two be in one bed; the one shall be taken, and the other left.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Though thou be of compulsion put to grind, whether thou be man or woman; though thou be in fetters, and sit beside the mill, yet He who by His might bringeth out them that are bound, will not overlook thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He who brought forth Joseph out of slavery and prison to a kingdom, shall redeem thee also from thy afflictions into the kingdom of heaven.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Only be of good cheer, only work, only strive earnestly; for nothing shall be lost.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Every prayer of thine, every Psalm thou singest is recorded; every alms-deed, every fast is recorded; every marriage duly observed is recorded; continence kept for God’s sake is recorded; but the first crowns in the records are those of virginity and purity; and thou shalt shine as an Angel.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But as thou hast gladly listened to the good things, so listen again without shrinking to the contrary.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Every covetous deed of thine is recorded; thine every act of fornication is recorded, thine every false oath is recorded, every blasphemy, and sorcery, and theft, and murder.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: All these things are henceforth to be recorded, if thou do the same now after having been baptized; for thy former deeds are blotted out.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: When the Son of Man, He says, shall come in His glory, and all the Angels with Him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Behold, O man, before what multitudes thou shalt come to judgment.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Every race of mankind will then be present.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Reckon, therefore, how many are the Roman nation; reckon how many the barbarian tribes now living, and how many have died within the last hundred years; reckon how many nations have been buried during the last thousand years; reckon all from Adam to this day.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Great indeed is the multitude; but yet it is little, for the Angels are many more.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They are the ninety and nine sheep, but mankind is the single one.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For according to the extent of universal space, must we reckon the number of its inhabitants.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The whole earth is but as a point in the midst of the one heaven, and yet contains so great a multitude; what a multitude must the heaven which encircles it contain?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And must not the heaven of heavens contain unimaginable numbers?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And it is written, Thousand thousands ministered unto Him, and ten thousand times ten thousand stood before Him; not that the multitude is only so great, but because the Prophet could not express more than these.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: So there will be present at the judgment in that day, God, the Father of all, Jesus Christ being seated with Him, and the Holy Ghost present with Them; and an angel’s trumpet shall summon us all to bring our deeds with us.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Ought we not then from this time forth to be sore troubled?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Think it not a slight doom, O man, even apart from punishment, to be condemned in the presence of so many.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Shall we not choose rather to die many deaths, than be condemned by friends?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us dread then, brethren, lest God condemn us; who needs not examination or proofs, to condemn.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Say not, In the night I committed fornication, or wrought sorcery, or did any other thing, and there was no man by.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Out of thine own conscience shalt thou be judged, thy thoughts the meanwhile accusing or else excusing, in the day when God shall judge the secrets of men.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The terrible countenance of the Judge will force thee to speak the truth; or rather, even though thou speak not, it will convict thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For thou shalt rise clothed with thine own sins, or else with thy righteous deeds.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And this has the Judge Himself declared—for it is Christ who judges—for neither doth the Father judge any man, but he hath given all judgment unto the Son, not divesting Himself of His power, but judging through the Son; the Son therefore judgeth by the will of the Father; for the wills of the Father and of the Son are not different, but one and the same.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What then says the Judge, as to whether thou shalt bear thy works, or no?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And before Him shall they gather all nations: (for in the presence of Christ every knee must bow, of things in heaven, and things in earth, and things under the earth:) and He shall separate them one from another, as the shepherd divideth his sheep from the goats.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: How does the shepherd make the separation?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Does he examine out of a book which is a sheep and which a goat? or does he distinguish by their evident marks?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Does not the wool show the sheep, and the hairy and rough skin the goat?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In like manner, if thou hast been just now cleansed from thy sins, thy deeds shall be henceforth as pure wool; and thy robe shall remain unstained, and thou shalt ever say, I have put off my coat, how shall I put it on?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: By thy vesture shalt thou be known for a sheep.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But if thou be found hairy, like Esau, who was rough with hair, and wicked in mind, who for food lost his birthright and sold his privilege, thou shalt be one of those on the left hand.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But God forbid that any here present should be cast out from grace, or for evil deeds be found among the ranks of the sinners on the left hand!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Terrible in good truth is the judgment, and terrible the things announced.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The kingdom of heaven is set before us, and everlasting fire is prepared.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: How then, some one will say, are we to escape the fire?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And how to enter into the kingdom?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I was an hungered, He says, and ye gave Me meat.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Learn hence the way; there is here no need of allegory, but to fulfil what is said.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I was an hungered, and ye gave Me meat; I was thirsty, and ye gave Me drink; I was a stranger, and ye took Me in; naked, and ye clothed Me; I was sick, and ye visited Me; I was in prison, and ye came unto Me.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: These things if thou do, thou shalt reign together with Him; but if thou do them not, thou shalt be condemned.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: At once then begin to do these works, and abide in the faith; lest, like the foolish virgins, tarrying to buy oil, thou be shut out.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Be not confident because thou merely possessest the lamp, but constantly keep it burning.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let the light of thy good works shine before men, and let not Christ be blasphemed on thy account.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Wear thou a garment of incorruption, resplendent in good works; and whatever matter thou receivest from God to administer as a steward, administer profitably.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hast thou been put in trust with riches?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Dispense them well.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hast thou been entrusted with the word of teaching?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Be a good steward thereof.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Canst thou attach the souls of the hearers?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Do this diligently.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: There are many doors of good stewardship.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Only let none of us be condemned and cast out; that we may with boldness meet Christ the Everlasting King, who reigns for ever.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He doth reign for ever, who shall be judge of quick and dead, because for quick and dead He died.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And as Paul says, For to this end Christ both died and lived again, that He might be Lord both of the dead and living.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And shouldest thou ever hear any say that the kingdom of Christ shall have an end, abhor the heresy; it is another head of the dragon, lately sprung up in .

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A certain one has dared to affirm, that after the end of the world Christ shall reign no longer; he has also dared to say, that the Word having come forth from the Father shall be again absorbed into the Father, and shall be no more; uttering such blasphemies to his own perdition.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For he has not listened to the Lord, saying, The Son abideth for ever.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He has not listened to Gabriel, saying, And He shall reign over the house of Jacob for ever, and of His kingdom there shall be no end.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Consider this text.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Heretics of this day teach in disparagement of Christ, while Gabriel the Archangel taught the eternal abiding of the Saviour; whom then wilt thou rather believe? wilt thou not rather give credence to Gabriel?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Listen to the testimony of Daniel in the text; I saw in a vision of the night, and behold, one like the Son of Man came with the clouds of heaven, and came to the Ancient of days.….And to Him was given the honour, and the dominion, and the kingdom: and all peoples, tribes, and languages shall serve Him; His dominion is an everlasting dominion, which shall not pass away, and His kingdom shall not be destroyed.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: These things rather hold fast, these things believe, and cast away from thee the words of heresy; for thou hast heard most plainly of the endless kingdom of Christ.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The like doctrine thou has also in the interpretation of the Stone, which was cut out of a mountain without hands, which is Christ according to the flesh; And His kingdom shall not be left to another people.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: David also says in one place, Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever; and in another place, Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the foundations of the earth, &c., they shall perish, but Thou remainest, &c.; but Thou art the same, and Thy years shall not fail: words which Paul has interpreted of the Son.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And wouldest thou know how they who teach the contrary ran into such madness?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They read wrongly that good word of the Apostle, For He must reign, till He hath put all enemies under His feet; and they say, when His enemies shall have been put under His feet, He shall cease to reign, wrongly and foolishly alleging this.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He who is king before He has subdued His enemies, how shall He not the rather be king, after He has gotten the mastery over them.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They have also dared to say that the Scripture, When all things shall be subjected unto Him, then shall the Son also Himself be subjected unto Him that subjected all things unto Him,—that this Scripture shews that the Son also shall be absorbed into the Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Shall ye then, O most impious of all men, ye the creatures of Christ, continue? and shall Christ perish, by whom both you and all things were made?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Such a word is blasphemous.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But further, how shall all things be made subject unto Him?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: By perishing, or by abiding?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Shall then the other things, when subject to the Son abide, and shall the Son, when subject to the Father, not abide?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He shall be subjected, not because He shall then begin to do the Father’s will (for from eternity He doth always those things that please Him), but because, then as before, He obeys the Father, yielding, not a forced obedience, but a self-chosen accordance; for He is not a servant, that He should be subjected by force, but a Son, that He should comply of His free choice and natural love.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But let us examine them; what is the meaning of “until” or “as long as?”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For with the very phrase will I close with them, and try to overthrow their error.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Since they have dared to say that the words, till He hath put His enemies under His feet, shew that He Himself shall have an end, and have presumed to set bounds to the eternal kingdom of Christ, and to bring to an end, as far as words go, His never-ending sovereignty, come then, let us read the like expressions in the Apostle: Nevertheless, death reigned from Adam till Moses.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Did men then die up to that time, and did none die any more after Moses, or after the Law has there been no more death among men?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Well then, thou seest that the word “unto” is not to limit time; but that Paul rather signified this,—“And yet, though Moses was a righteous and wonderful man, nevertheless the doom of death, which was uttered against Adam, reached even unto him, and them that came after him; and this, though they had not committed the like sins as Adam, by his disobedience in eating of the tree.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Take again another similar text.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For until this day…when Moses is read, a vail lieth upon their heart.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Does until this day mean only “until Paul?”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is it not until this day present, and even to the end?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And if Paul say to the Corinthians, For we came even as far as unto you in preaching the Gospel of Christ, having hope when your faith increases to preach the Gospel in the regions beyond you, thou seest manifestly that as far as implies not the end, but has something following it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In what sense then shouldest thou remember that Scripture, till He hath put all enemies under His feet?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: According as Paul says in another place, And exhort each other daily, while it is called to-day; meaning, “continually.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For as we may not speak of the “beginning of the days” of Christ, so neither suffer thou that any should ever speak of the end of His kingdom.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For it is written, His kingdom is an everlasting kingdom.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And though I have many more testimonies out of the divine Scriptures, concerning the kingdom of Christ which has no end for ever, I will be content at present with those above mentioned, because the day is far spent.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But thou, O hearer, worship only Him as thy King, and flee all heretical error.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And if the grace of God permit us, the remaining Articles also of the Faith shall be in good time declared to you.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And may the God of the whole world keep you all in safety, bearing in mind the signs of the end, and remaining unsubdued by Antichrist.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast received the tokens of the Deceiver who is to come; thou hast received the proofs of the true Christ, who shall openly come down from heaven.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Flee therefore the one, the False one; and look for the other, the True.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast learnt the way, how in the judgment thou mayest be found among those on the right hand; guard that which is committed to thee concerning Christ, and be conspicuous in good works, that thou mayest stand with a good confidence before the Judge, and inherit the kingdom of heaven:—Through whom, and with whom, be glory to God with the Holy Ghost, for ever and ever.
